_note: Curated Bourbon & Cigars set for The GuyTalk Guide. Prices are representative (mid-2026) and must be verified at link time. For each item: 'productSearch' is the exact string CC should use to find the right product + official image; 'preferredRetailer' is where to deep-link; 'linkType' is 'specific' (durable, deep-link the product) or 'category-fallback' (allocated/hard-to-find — link the retailer's brand/category page so it doesn't die). 'image' is blank for CC to download + host. Bios are final, in GuyTalk voice.
categoryTagline: How to drink it, what to pour for guests, and what to actually buy.

picks.name: Kentucky Straight Bourbon
picks.bio: The bottle that should always be in your house. It's smooth, balanced, mixes well, and sips fine neat — there is no bourbon that does more for $30. If a guy asks where to start, the answer is here, full stop.
picks.flashyCheck: Zero flash, total credibility. Pouring Buffalo Trace says you actually know, not that you overspent.

picks.name: 101 Proof Bourbon
picks.bio: Higher proof, bigger flavor, still under thirty bucks. This is the bottle that teaches you what bourbon is supposed to taste like with the volume turned up. Great in an Old Fashioned because the proof stands up to the ice and sugar instead of disappearing.
picks.flashyCheck: None. This is a bartender's bourbon — knowing it marks you as someone who drinks, not someone who poses.

picks.name: Single Barrel
picks.bio: The step-up bottle for when you want to taste the difference. Higher proof, more complexity, and a price that still makes sense for a Tuesday. This is the one to pour when someone says they don't really like bourbon — it changes minds.
picks.flashyCheck: Safe and quietly impressive. Reads 'has taste,' not 'showing off.'

picks.name: 10 Year Single Barrel
picks.bio: Ten years old for around forty-five dollars at sticker price — which is why it's almost never on the shelf. If you spot it at MSRP, grab it; it punches like a bottle twice the price. Don't pay the crazy secondary markup, though — that's where the value evaporates.
picks.flashyCheck: The hunt is the only flashy part. The bottle itself is pure substance.

picks.name: Original Single Barrel
picks.bio: The bottle that looks as good as it tastes — the round decanter and the little horse-and-jockey stoppers are why it lives on the top shelf at every bar trying to impress. It earns the spot. Just know it's allocated, so expect a hunt or a markup.
picks.flashyCheck: This one IS a flex — but a tasteful one. It's the bottle people recognize without it being obnoxious.

picks.name: 23 Year — The Overhyped One
picks.bio: Here's the honest take nobody at the liquor store will give you: Pappy is very good, but you're paying thousands for the name and the chase, not thousands of dollars of better whiskey. Want the Van Winkle profile without the insanity? Weller 12 is from the same distillery and the same mashbill — find it near retail and you've basically cracked the code.
picks.flashyCheck: Chasing Pappy is the flashiest move in bourbon. Knowing to buy Weller 12 instead is the actual flex.

picks.name: Serie V Cigar
picks.bio: A full-bodied, rich cigar that drinks well above its price — the one to hand a friend when you want them to think you really know cigars. Pairs perfectly with a higher-proof bourbon. If you're buying one box to learn on, make it this.
picks.flashyCheck: Safe. Knowing Oliva over the airport-humidor names signals real interest, not a one-time golf-trip purchase.
picks.preferredRetailer: Famous Smoke Shop or Cigars International

picks.name: Hemingway Short Story
picks.bio: A smooth, approachable, perfectly-made cigar that almost nobody dislikes — the safe pick for a group where not everyone smokes regularly. The short size means you're not committing to two hours. This is the cigar to keep on hand for celebrations.
picks.flashyCheck: None. Classic and well-loved — a quiet sign you've done this before.
picks.productSearch: Arturo Fuente Hemingway Short Story cigar
picks.preferredRetailer: Famous Smoke Shop or JR Cigar

picks.name: Whisky Tasting Glass (Set of 2)
picks.bio: The glass that actually makes whiskey taste better by funneling the aroma to your nose. Twenty-five bucks for the set, and it instantly makes you look like you take this seriously. The cheapest upgrade to your home bar by a mile.
picks.flashyCheck: None — it's a tasting tool, not a prop. Pulling these out reads as genuine interest.

picks.name: King Cube Silicone Ice Tray
picks.bio: Big, slow-melting cubes that chill your pour without watering it down in five minutes — the single detail that makes a home Old Fashioned feel like a bar one. Twelve dollars, lasts forever. Skip the gimmicky sphere molds; the square cube is the move.
picks.flashyCheck: None. It's a quiet upgrade that guests always notice and never expect at someone's house.
